# Theological Guidelines

Islam.wiki follows Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jamaah methodology. All content adheres to these principles:

## Source Priority

1. Quran (primary source)
2. Authentic Hadith (Sahih and Hasan)
3. Consensus of the Companions (Ijma al-Sahabah)
4. Classical scholarly consensus
5. Established positions of the four madhabs

## Madhab Preference Order

When presenting fiqh positions: Hanbali, Shafi'i, Maliki, Hanafi, Dhahiri.

All four madhabs are respected and presented. The preference order affects default display, not exclusion.

## Content Standards

- Every claim must have a source reference
- Hadith must include grading (Sahih, Hasan, Da'if)
- Disputed matters present multiple scholarly views
- No sectarian bias in encyclopedic content
- Historical accuracy prioritized over popular narratives
